title: Tender Toasted German Yeast Pancakes
description: A traditional German dish featuring yeasty, aerated pancakes toasted on a comal and served with sweetened condensed milk, perfect for a post-workout snack. This lacto-vegetarian-friendly recipe is easy to make and delicious to eat.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, yeast, sugar, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the warm milk, eggs, and melted butter.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until a smooth batter forms.
Knead the batter for 5 minutes until it becomes elastic and smooth.
Place the batter in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat a comal or griddle over medium heat.
Once the comal is hot, use a 1/4 cup measuring cup to scoop the batter onto the comal.
Cook the pancakes for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and toasted.
Serve the pancakes hot with sweetened condensed milk drizzled on top.
